---
title: "Statistics & ML"
---

Modelling stacks relevant to biomarker discovery, multi-omics integration, and survival analysis. Curated for the trauma / tissue-injury context — heavy on regularised models, mixed effects, and Bayesian workflows.

## Multi-omics integration

::: {.resource-entry}
### [mixOmics](http://mixomics.org/)
License: OSS · GPL-3.0  ·  Maintainer: Kim-Anh Lê Cao et al.

Sparse multivariate methods for omics integration: PLS-DA, sPLS, DIABLO, MINT. The de-facto package when the question is "what jointly varies across two or more omics blocks?"
:::

## Modelling frameworks

::: {.resource-entry}
### [tidymodels](https://www.tidymodels.org/)
License: OSS · MIT  ·  Maintainer: Posit

Modern, opinionated modelling meta-package. Resampling, tuning, recipe-based preprocessing, and a unified `parsnip` interface across model engines.
:::

::: {.resource-entry}
### [mlr3](https://mlr3.mlr-org.com/)
License: OSS · LGPL-3.0  ·  Maintainer: mlr-org

Object-oriented machine-learning framework with strong support for nested resampling, pipelines, and benchmarking. The choice when method comparison or pipeline composition is the central concern.
:::

::: {.resource-entry}
### [caret](https://topepo.github.io/caret/)
License: OSS · GPL-2.0 / GPL-3.0  ·  Maintainer: Max Kuhn

Predecessor to `tidymodels`. Still widely used in legacy code and books; new projects should default to `tidymodels`.
:::

## Specific model families

::: {.resource-entry}
### [glmnet](https://glmnet.stanford.edu/)
License: OSS · GPL-2.0  ·  Maintainer: Trevor Hastie / Jerome Friedman et al.

Lasso, ridge, and elastic-net generalised linear models. The reference implementation; cited in essentially every regularised-regression methods paper.
:::

::: {.resource-entry}
### [randomForest](https://cran.r-project.org/package=randomForest) · [ranger](https://imbs-hl.github.io/ranger/)
License: OSS · GPL-2.0 / GPL-3.0  ·  Maintainer: Liaw / Wright

`ranger` is the fast, multithreaded reimplementation; reach for it when row counts get into six figures.
:::

::: {.resource-entry}
### [xgboost](https://xgboost.readthedocs.io/)
License: OSS · Apache-2.0  ·  Maintainer: DMLC

Gradient-boosted trees. Native R interface; the workhorse for tabular prediction baselines.
:::

## Survival analysis

::: {.resource-entry}
### [survival](https://cran.r-project.org/package=survival)
License: OSS · LGPL-2.0  ·  Maintainer: Terry Therneau

The reference R survival library. Cox regression, parametric survival, frailty models, multi-state models — the canonical implementation.
:::

::: {.resource-entry}
### [survminer](https://rpkgs.datanovia.com/survminer/)
License: OSS · GPL-2.0  ·  Maintainer: Alboukadel Kassambara

`ggplot2` Kaplan–Meier plots, forest plots, and diagnostic plots for `survival` and related models. Almost mandatory for publication-ready survival figures.
:::

## Mixed-effects and Bayesian

::: {.resource-entry}
### [lme4](https://cran.r-project.org/package=lme4)
License: OSS · GPL-2.0  ·  Maintainer: Douglas Bates et al.

Linear and generalised linear mixed-effects models. The default frequentist mixed-model engine in R.
:::

::: {.resource-entry}
### [Stan](https://mc-stan.org/) · [brms](https://paul-buerkner.github.io/brms/) · [rstanarm](https://mc-stan.org/rstanarm/)
License: OSS · BSD-3-Clause / GPL-2.0+  ·  Maintainer: Stan development team

Probabilistic programming for full-Bayesian inference. `brms` provides a `lme4`-flavoured formula interface that compiles to Stan; `rstanarm` precompiles common GLM/GLMM templates.
:::
